section01.title: Foundations
section01.summary: Core knowledge that makes everything else work: history, vocabulary, type, color, layout.

unit01.title: Design History & Foundations
unit01.summary: From hand-lettered manuscripts to movable type and modern design. Students learn core vocabulary and how design solves communication problems.
unit01.description: A fast, engaging survey: illuminated manuscripts, the printing press, early typesetting, Arts & Crafts, Bauhaus/Swiss styles, and the digital era. Emphasis on how form serves function and how to talk about design choices.

chapters.title: Before Print
chapters.summary: Scribes, illuminated letters, hierarchy by scale and color.

chapters.title: Movable Type
chapters.summary: Gutenberg, typesetting basics, early page layout.

chapters.title: Modern Movements
chapters.summary: Bauhaus/Swiss—grids, clarity, universal type ideals.

chapters.title: Design Vocabulary
chapters.summary: Elements & principles; critique language and norms.

unit02.title: Typography Fundamentals
unit02.summary: Students learn type anatomy, serif vs. sans, spacing, pairing, and readability—then apply hierarchy on a grid.
unit02.description: Paper-first drills on x-height, cap height, kerning, tracking, leading; serif vs. sans; expressive vs. functional type. Students build clear Title→Subhead→Body hierarchy and practice peer critique using the squint test.

chapters.title: Type Anatomy & Families
chapters.summary: Baseline, x-height, ascenders/descenders; serif vs. sans.

chapters.title: Spacing Systems
chapters.summary: Kerning, tracking, leading—optical vs. equal spacing.

chapters.title: Hierarchy on a Grid
chapters.summary: Size/weight/space to control first, second, third read.

chapters.title: Type Pairing
chapters.summary: Role-based pairing; contrast without conflict.

unit03.title: Color & Contrast
unit03.summary: Students use hue, value, saturation, and limited palettes to support mood and readability.
unit03.description: Hands-on with color schemes, value contrast, and accessibility-minded choices. Students build small brand palettes and test legibility with squint and distance checks.

chapters.title: Hue/Value/Saturation
chapters.summary: How value drives readability more than hue.

chapters.title: Palettes & Schemes
chapters.summary: Analogous, complementary, split-complementary, triads.

chapters.title: Contrast for Clarity
chapters.summary: Light/dark control; avoiding low-contrast traps.

unit04.title: Layout, Grids & Visual Hierarchy
unit04.summary: Students organize information with margins, columns, and alignment patterns that guide the eye.
unit04.description: Grids (single column to simple modular), margin discipline, alignment, whitespace, F/Z patterns. Students create multi-level layouts that pass a one-second squint test.

chapters.title: Margins & Alignment
chapters.summary: Rag quality, left align vs. center pitfalls.

chapters.title: Simple Grids
chapters.summary: Columns, gutters, modules—when and why.

chapters.title: First-Read Patterns
chapters.summary: Scale, contrast, and placement to direct attention.

section02.title: Identity & Branding
section02.summary: Create a simple, legible logo and turn it into a small, consistent identity.

unit05.title: Logo Design: Marks & Wordmarks
unit05.summary: Students design simple, legible logos that still read when tiny or squinted.
unit05.description: Logo types (wordmark, lettermark, symbol), reduction and simplicity, consistent stroke modules. B/W first, then small-size tests.

chapters.title: Idea Thumbnails
chapters.summary: 20-in-10 divergent sketching for quantity of ideas.

chapters.title: Reduction & Consistency
chapters.summary: Stroke modules, counters, corner logic.

chapters.title: Black & White Master
chapters.summary: Clarity without color; min-size and squint tests.

unit06.title: Brand Kit Basics
unit06.summary: Students turn a logo into a small identity system with color, clear space, and usage rules.
unit06.description: Build a mini brand guide: primary mark, clear-space rule, minimum size, two-color palette (+ neutral), and simple do/don’t guidelines with examples.

chapters.title: Clear Space & Minimum Size
chapters.summary: Protecting the mark; grid-based rules.

chapters.title: Color Application
chapters.summary: Primary/accent color; when to use B/W.

chapters.title: Do/Don’t System
chapters.summary: Avoid stretching, outlines, busy backgrounds; good placement.

section03.title: Communication & Production
section03.summary: Apply hierarchy and brand rules to print and digital deliverables; practice clean information design.

unit07.title: Print Communication: Poster & Flyer
unit07.summary: Students apply hierarchy and brand rules to a print piece that communicates clearly.
unit07.description: Poster or flyer with strong title, clean body copy, and visible CTA. Basics of print sizing, margins/bleed concepts, and export mindsets (even if final is paper).

chapters.title: Purpose & Audience
chapters.summary: Briefs that define who, what, where, when, why.

chapters.title: Composition & CTA
chapters.summary: Space priority; getting the action noticed.

chapters.title: Refine & Crit
chapters.summary: Peer review with squint, distance, and checklist.

unit08.title: Digital Communication: Social Graphics
unit08.summary: Students adapt designs to common aspect ratios and keep identity consistent across posts.
unit08.description: Square/post/story/header formats, safe areas, text sizing for mobile, and rapid series design while maintaining brand voice.

chapters.title: Format & Scale
chapters.summary: What changes across sizes; what must stay consistent.

chapters.title: Series & Tempo
chapters.summary: 3-post sets with consistent hierarchy and style.

chapters.title: Alt Text & Clarity
chapters.summary: Writing concise, helpful alt text and captions.

unit09.title: Information Design: Icons & Infographics
unit09.summary: Students translate data and ideas into simple visuals that are quick to read.
unit09.description: Icon sketching with basic geometry, simple charts and comparisons, and layout that favors scanning.

chapters.title: Icon Basics
chapters.summary: Stroke consistency; pixel/graph logic; recognizable silhouettes.

chapters.title: Visualizing Data
chapters.summary: When to use bars, pies, pictograms; labeling that helps.

chapters.title: Clutter Control
chapters.summary: Remove decoration; emphasize meaning.

section04.title: Professional Practice & Portfolio
section04.summary: Work ethically, credit sources, and present a portfolio that explains choices and results.

unit10.title: Ethics, Sourcing & Credits
unit10.summary: Students learn to use images, type, and symbols responsibly and give proper credit.
unit10.description: Copyright & fair use at a middle-school level, Creative Commons basics, citing sources, and avoiding distortion or misuse of school marks.

chapters.title: Copyright & CC
chapters.summary: What’s allowed; how to attribute simply.

chapters.title: School Identity Use
chapters.summary: Clear rules for logos, colors, and spirit graphics.

chapters.title: Reflections on Impact
chapters.summary: Design fairness, accessibility, and audience respect.

unit11.title: Portfolio & Showcase
unit11.summary: Students curate, explain, and present their best work to show growth and skill.
unit11.description: Select projects, write short reflections (purpose, decisions, outcomes), and prepare a simple exhibit or slide deck—emphasis on vocabulary and critique readiness.

chapters.title: Curate & Sequence
chapters.summary: Pick pieces that show range and improvement.

chapters.title: Write Captions
chapters.summary: Purpose, choices, results—in 2–3 sentences each.

chapters.title: Present & Reflect
chapters.summary: Peer critique, revisions, and final share-out.
